{{Quote|Call me Sosakar.|First line of ''The Slug Named Grendel''|<ref name="Slug" />}}
'''''The Slug Named Grendel''''' was a famous work by noted author [[Rogar Farnoster]], published by [[TriPlanetary Press/Legends|TriPlanetary Press]]<ref name="Slug" /> sometime after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]].<ref name="HNN54">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|54|life/1352_2.html|Giant Space Slug Found}}</ref> It is told from the point of view of [[Sosakar]], crewman aboard the ''[[Darkfire]]'' under the command of [[Flandon Sweeg]], as they pursue the great [[Exogorth/Legends|space slug]] [[Grendel (space slug)|Grendel]] in the dangerous [[Borkeen Belt]].<ref name="Slug" />

Farnoster researched the novel by accompanying Professor [[Sedan Anang]] and other [[University of Sanbra]] scientists when they investigated a record-breaking 80-meter space slug discovered in the Borkeen Belt shortly before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out.<ref name="HNN54" />

The book may have been inspired by older stories of great slugs, such as one recalled by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ares Nune/Legends|Ares Nune]] while hunting for a mysterious [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Malevolence/Legends|superweapon]].<ref name="Shakedown">''[[The Clone Wars: Shakedown]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The work bears strong similarities with the 1851 novel [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moby_Dick Moby Dick], authored by [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Herman_Melville Herman Melville]. Such similarities includes the formatting of the first line and the narrative of the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ishmael_(Moby-Dick) protagonist] who has joined a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pequod_(Moby-Dick) crew] upon a quest led by a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Captain_Ahab captain] of questionable morals after a sizable [[Limb/Legends|limbless]], [[Starship/Legends|ship]]-destroying [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moby_Dick_(whale) creature] [[Color/Legends|colored]] white.
